title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders. They do NOT represent pre-colonial Pacific sovereignties (Tu'i Tonga maritime empire, Samoan matai system, Ni-Vanuatu island councils) or Austronesian voyaging routes that linked these peoples for 3,000+ years.

Vanuatu (modern borders) — kava's birthplace
description: Modern political border (post-1980 independence). ORIGIN of Piper methysticum — Tanna and Efate islands. World's largest producer of noble kava. 'Nakamal' is Ni-Vanuatu in origin, now pan-Pacific.

Fiji (modern borders) — yaqona ceremony + tanoa bowl
description: Modern political border (post-1970 independence). Fijian yaqona ceremony is a distinct cultural tradition; tanoa wooden bowl is a Fijian invention. Second-largest exporter today.

Samoa (modern borders) — 'ava + Fa'a Samoa
description: Modern political border (post-1962 independence). ''ava ceremony integrates with matai chieftaincy system — removing the ceremony would unmake the political structure. American Samoa is a separate US territory east of here.

Tonga (modern borders) — Tu'i Tonga sacred ceremony
description: Modern political border (never fully colonised — unique among Pacific nations). Tongan kava ceremony is among the most formally codified. Tu'i Tonga sacred-king ritual is documented by Lebot, Merlin & Lindstrom (1992).

Hawai'i (US modern borders) — 'awa reclamation
description: Modern political border (US state since 1959; sovereignty disputes ongoing). Kānaka maoli ('awa cultivation was nearly extinguished by missionary suppression; kava revival movement is part of broader Hawaiian cultural resurgence.

USA (modern borders) — Pacific diaspora + kava bar culture
description: Modern political border. Florida has the world's highest density of kava bars outside the Pacific. California, Washington, Utah, and Texas also anchor US diaspora communities.

Australia + New Zealand (modern borders) — largest Pacific diaspora
description: Modern political borders. Auckland NZ has the largest Pacific Islander urban population in the world. Australia hosts significant Fijian and Samoan communities. Kava ceremony maintained as diaspora cultural practice.
